title: nvm_write auto-assign path: caller cannot recover the assigned NvmId
description: When nvm_write is called with NvmId::INVALID (0), wolfHSM auto-assigns an ID and the call returns Ok(()). The assigned ID is not returned. To discover it, the caller must call nvm_list() and infer which ID is new — a racy operation if multiple threads or clients share the HSM. This implicit constraint (don't use NvmId::INVALID unless you don't need to reference the object again) is undiscoverable from the function signature. Consequence: users who follow the happy path write data they can never read back, then file bugs. Fix: return the assigned NvmId from nvm_write, or split into nvm_add (returns Id) and nvm_overwrite (takes existing Id).
